entry.title: Outlook
entry.content:
Outlook

Purpose

Project/task outlook (based on RAG).

License

Published by Currawong Games Interactive under CC0 1.0 Universal — no rights reserved.

Download

JSON-LD
outlook.skos.jsonld
Turtle
outlook.skos.ttl

Preview

ConceptLabelDefinitionValueRangeColour
outlook:on-trackOn TrackTrajectory is within tolerance. No intervention required.1[0.5, 1.5)
outlook:at-riskAt RiskTrajectory is approaching tolerance boundary. Early warning — monitor closely and consider corrective action.2[1.5, 2.5)
outlook:off-trackOff TrackTrajectory is outside tolerance or requires immediate intervention.3[2.5, 3.5)

Implementation notes

Defining the "At Risk" space between "On Track" and "Off Track"

Implementors are free to choose their own method for calculating boundaries, based on their risk calculations and specific needs. One suggestion drawn from project management practices is to use estimates and tolerances. On track is within estimates (inclusive). At risk is outside the estimate but with tolerance. Off track is outside tolerance.

Integer or floating-point numbers?

The transitions between values are often the most important information for end users. Floating point values allow changes to be tracked over time rather than rounding off in a lossy manner. A project that was tracking at 1.0 ("On Track") last week and is now 1.3 is still "On Track", but it may be useful to know about the change before it breaches 1.5 ("At Risk").

Values SHOULD be checked against the range using minimum (inclusive) and maximum (exclusive) bounds:

// [min, max) — min inclusive, max exclusive
value >= min && value < max

  • The canonical integer values (1, 2, 3) sit at the centre of each concept's range and are sufficient for most use cases. Implementations MAY choose to use integers only.
  • Values cross into the next concept at the boundary. A value of 1.5 is "At Risk", not "On Track".
  • Considering floating point values:
    • allows users to organise items via drag and drop, placing items relative to each other based on their knowledge.
    • can calculate roll up status based on multiple child items for greater granularity in reporting.
  • 0 up to 0.5 (exclusive) is not defined. This is intentional to allow 1 to sit consistently in the middle of the range.
  • Values below 0.5 or above 3.5 fall outside the defined range of this vocabulary.
  • Colours are illustrative only. Implementors SHOULD choose colours (if any) based on usability and accessibility.

JSON-LD

{
  "@context": {
    "skos":    "http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#",
    "dc":      "http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/",
    "dct":     "http://purl.org/dc/terms/",
    "xsd":     "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#",

    "cgi":     "https://currawong.games/ns#",
    "rag":     "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag#",

    "title":         { "@id": "dc:title" },
    "identifier":    { "@id": "dc:identifier" },
    "description":   { "@id": "dct:description" },
    "creator":       { "@id": "dct:creator", "@type": "@id" },
    "license":       { "@id": "dct:license", "@type": "@id" },
    "hasTopConcept": { "@id": "skos:hasTopConcept", "@type": "@id" },
    "inScheme":      { "@id": "skos:inScheme", "@type": "@id" },
    "topConceptOf":  { "@id": "skos:topConceptOf", "@type": "@id" },
    "prefLabel":     { "@id": "skos:prefLabel", "@language": "en" },
    "definition":    { "@id": "skos:definition", "@language": "en" },
    "scopeNote":     { "@id": "skos:scopeNote", "@language": "en" },
    "notation":      { "@id": "skos:notation" },

    "rangeMin":      { "@id": "cgi:rangeMin", "@type": "xsd:decimal" },
    "rangeMax":      { "@id": "cgi:rangeMax", "@type": "xsd:decimal" }
  },
  "@graph": [
    {
      "@id": "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag",
      "@type": "skos:ConceptScheme",
      "identifier": "rag",
      "title": "RAG Status",
      "description": "A three-value ordinal scale — Red, Amber, Green — used as a traffic-light signal for condition, tolerance, or risk level. The colours are intrinsic to the concept identifiers: red means stop/intervene, amber means caution/monitor, green means proceed/within tolerance.\n\nValues are floating-point numbers. The integer part identifies the concept via range lookup. The fractional part expresses relative position within that concept — proximity to a boundary may be used to signal threshold drift, but the interpretation of position is intentionally left to the consuming application.\n\nThis is an abstract base vocabulary. Domain-specific schemes (e.g. cgi:Outlook) use skos:exactMatch to map their concepts onto these three values.",
      "creator": "https://currawong.games",
      "license": "https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/",
      "hasTopConcept": [
        "rag:red",
        "rag:amber",
        "rag:green"
      ]
    },
    {
      "@id": "rag:red",
      "@type": "skos:Concept",
      "inScheme": "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag",
      "topConceptOf": "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag",
      "prefLabel": "Red",
      "notation": "R",
      "definition": "Outside tolerance or requires immediate intervention.",
      "scopeNote": "Fractional values approaching 2.5 indicate drift toward the amber boundary.",
      "rangeMin": 2.5,
      "rangeMax": 3.5
    },
    {
      "@id": "rag:amber",
      "@type": "skos:Concept",
      "inScheme": "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag",
      "topConceptOf": "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag",
      "prefLabel": "Amber",
      "notation": "A",
      "definition": "Approaching tolerance boundary. Early warning. Monitor closely and consider action.",
      "scopeNote": "Fractional values approaching 2.5 indicate drift toward red; values approaching 1.5 indicate drift toward green.",
      "rangeMin": 1.5,
      "rangeMax": 2.5
    },
    {
      "@id": "rag:green",
      "@type": "skos:Concept",
      "inScheme": "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag",
      "topConceptOf": "https://currawong.games/vocab/rag",
      "prefLabel": "Green",
      "notation": "G",
      "definition": "Within tolerance. No intervention required. Continue monitoring.",
      "scopeNote": "Fractional values approaching 1.5 indicate drift toward the amber boundary.",
      "rangeMin": 0.5,
      "rangeMax": 1.5
    }
  ]
}

Turtle

@prefix skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>.
@prefix dc: <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>.
@prefix dct: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/>.
@prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>.
@prefix cgi: <https://currawong.games/ns#>.
@prefix rag: <https://currawong.games/vocab/rag#>.

rag:amber a skos:Concept;
    skos:definition "Approaching tolerance boundary. Early warning. Monitor closely and consider action."@en;
    skos:inScheme <https://currawong.games/vocab/rag>;
    skos:notation "A";
    skos:prefLabel "Amber"@en;
    skos:scopeNote "Fractional values approaching 2.5 indicate drift toward red; values approaching 1.5 indicate drift toward green."@en;
    skos:topConceptOf <https://currawong.games/vocab/rag>;
    cgi:rangeMax "2.5E0"^^xsd:decimal;
    cgi:rangeMin "1.5E0"^^xsd:decimal.
<https://currawong.games/vocab/rag> a skos:ConceptScheme;
    dc:identifier "rag";
    dc:title "RAG Status";
    dct:creator <https://currawong.games>;
    dct:description "A three-value ordinal scale — Red, Amber, Green — used as a traffic-light signal for condition, tolerance, or risk level. The colours are intrinsic to the concept identifiers: red means stop/intervene, amber means caution/monitor, green means proceed/within tolerance.\n\nValues are floating-point numbers. The integer part identifies the concept via range lookup. The fractional part expresses relative position within that concept — proximity to a boundary may be used to signal threshold drift, but the interpretation of position is intentionally left to the consuming application.\n\nThis is an abstract base vocabulary. Domain-specific schemes (e.g. cgi:Outlook) use skos:exactMatch to map their concepts onto these three values.";
    dct:license <https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/>;
    skos:hasTopConcept rag:amber, rag:green, rag:red.
rag:green a skos:Concept;
    skos:definition "Within tolerance. No intervention required. Continue monitoring."@en;
    skos:inScheme <https://currawong.games/vocab/rag>;
    skos:notation "G";
    skos:prefLabel "Green"@en;
    skos:scopeNote "Fractional values approaching 1.5 indicate drift toward the amber boundary."@en;
    skos:topConceptOf <https://currawong.games/vocab/rag>;
    cgi:rangeMax "1.5E0"^^xsd:decimal;
    cgi:rangeMin "5.0E-1"^^xsd:decimal.
rag:red a skos:Concept;
    skos:definition "Outside tolerance or requires immediate intervention."@en;
    skos:inScheme <https://currawong.games/vocab/rag>;
    skos:notation "R";
    skos:prefLabel "Red"@en;
    skos:scopeNote "Fractional values approaching 2.5 indicate drift toward the amber boundary."@en;
    skos:topConceptOf <https://currawong.games/vocab/rag>;
    cgi:rangeMax "3.5E0"^^xsd:decimal;
    cgi:rangeMin "2.5E0"^^xsd:decimal.

entry.title: Our Services at Currawong Games Interactive
entry.summary: We offer multimedia, web and game design and development services.
entry.content:
Our Services

We'll help you build tools to make a difference in the world!

Contact us at hello@currawong.games

Creative workflow tools

Ben has over 10 years experience in creative workflow tools through working at Artella (Animation/Art review), Othelia Story Technologies (screenplay analysis and production), and best practice content management services.

Are your tools saving you time or are you jumping through hoops? Do your tools talk to each other, or do you have files and notes spread across fifty different apps?

  • Currawong Studio: digital asset management with integrated task tracking for pre-production workflows
  • Artella: art/animation review, feeds, file management and review workflows
  • Othelia: screenplay import/parsing, narrative analysis, story production management.

Civic Services

For government and non-profit organisations, we can help you build digital services that are accessible, user-friendly, and efficient.

Ben has 15+ years experience creating accessible solutions for Queensland Government, ranging across:

  • Community engagement (Get Involved platform redesign)
  • Complex forms (Application for Domestic Violence Prevention Order)
  • Self-service portals (Neighbourhood Dispute Resolution)
  • Content managment (template development and integration across multiple departments, platforms and third-party service providers)
  • Online payments (Qld Government Payment API: invoices, receipts, donations, shopping carts).

entry.title: Apps by Currawong Games Interactive
entry.content:
Our Apps

Creative tools for creative people.

Contact us at hello@currawong.games

entry.title: IncrediBass by Currawong Games Interactive
entry.summary: Personal software for the aspiring bassist. Track your practice sessions with easy access to your tab library, chord/fretboard charts and live sampling.
entry.content:
IncrediBass

Personal software for the aspiring bassist. Track your practice sessions with easy access to your tab library, chord/fretboard charts and live sampling.

What's your name again? Billy? Mac? Buddy! Bass Buddy!

My name is not Buddy! I'm IncrediBass!!

— Ben, quoting The Incredibles and Sheryl Crow badly.

Playing bass is fun. But operating a PC with a bass guitar over your shoulder is no easy feat. Optimised for bassists, IncrediBass is optimised to work with minimal input so you can keep playing. Important functions are a single key away!

Keyboard shortcuts
ShortcutDescriptionWhy?
SpacebarPlay/pauseStandard convention.
→Skip song (next!)Standard skip, no reason provided.
FSkip song (follow-up)You're skipping now but you want to circle back later. Flag it for follow up!
LSkip song (still learning)Don't know how to play this song yet? Skip it today with L.
MSkip song (mood)Not feeling it today? Skip it and stay in the groove!
TSkip song (wrong tuning)Didn't want to switch to drop-D mid-session? No worries, T skips the song.
VView tabWant a quick reminder how to play? Press V to view the tab.
5Nailed it!Rate your practice.
4Nailed it!Rate your practice.
3Nailed it!Rate your practice.
2Nailed it!Rate your practice.
1Nailed it!Rate your practice.

Features

Convenience while you play

  • Easy skip
  • Rate your playing
  • View tabs

Audio analysis

  • Live analysis from mic/line-in
  • Playback media tracking
  • Offline analysis for deep understanding of bass lines
  • Tackle a live challenge with hero mode!

Useful learning tools

  • Explore chords across the fretboard
  • Curate your play list
  • Review your practice strengths and weaknesses: what songs have you mastered? Which need more work?

Roadmap

StatusFeatureBenefit
✅Music detectionDetects currently playing media including artist, song, thumbnail and playback position.
✅Automatic tab loadingPoint it at your tab library (tabs not included). Matches on artist/song. Press V to View Tab.
Coming SoonHero mode(In Progress). Play against your past self — think you can nail it?

And more to come …

entry.title: Battleboats by Currawong Games Interactive
entry.summary: Battleboats is a friendly competition on the high seas, using realtime controls two players navigate a small map and attempt to outlast each other.
entry.content:
B2! You sunk my battleship!

Battleboats is a top-down, real-time, tactically-chaotic artillery game. You are given command of a battleship with a singular mission: sink all ships in the enemy's fleet. Pick from different classes of ships, go from the meagre battleship to destroyers and aircraft carriers. Deck out the deck with a variety of turrets.

Meet the Boats!
BattleshipDestroyerDreadnoughtAircraft CarrierRowboat
Nimble and fast, the battleship can quickly navigate the battlefield.Balancing speed and firepower, the destroyer is a versatile choice.A large battleship ready to dish out epic damage!Strike from the air and terrorise your opponents!An Old Salt Dog piloting a rowboat with his trusty shotgun. The ultimate challenge for skilled players!

Development

Future plans

  • Larger Battles (10v10)?
  • Different Playstyles (PvP, PvE, Escalation, Customizable!)
  • PvP & PvE
  • Solo Campaign
  • Skins
  • Attach things to the command tower for abilities?
  • Conning Tower
  • False Flag (disguise as enemy ship, or appear damaged?)

Inspired by games we love: Worms, Super Smash Bros, and of course, Battleship.

Battleboats is a top-down, real-time, tactically-chaotic artillery game. You are given command of a battleship with a singular mission: sink all ships in the enemy's fleet. Inspired by games we love: Worms, Super Smash Bros, and of course, Battleship.

B2! You sunk my battleship!

Battleboats is a top-down, real-time, tactically-chaotic artillery game. You are given command of a battleship with a singular mission: sink all ships in the enemy's fleet. Pick from different classes of ships, go from the meagre battleship to destroyers and aircraft carriers. Deck out the deck with a variety of turrets.

Nimble and fast, the battleship can quickly navigate the battlefield.Balancing speed and firepower, the destroyer is a versatile choice.A large battleship ready to dish out epic damage!Strike from the air and terrorise your opponents!An Old Salt Dog piloting a rowboat with his trusty shotgun. entry.title: Support Currawong Games
entry.summary: Support Currawong Games — indie games studio.
entry.content:
Customer Support

Need help? Drop us an email at support@currawong.games.

Support Currawong Games

Thanks for considering throwing some support our way! Every little bit helps.

Stay tuned for ways to support us!

entry.title: Places important to Currawong Games Interactive
entry.summary: feat(atom): add Atom feed generator for site updates
entry.content:
Where we're from

  • Upper Caboolture
  • Gubbi Gubbi Country

Our Worlds

Stay tuned …

entry.title: Upper Caboolture — Currawong Games Interactive
entry.content:
Upper Caboolture

Our studio is in Upper Caboolture, Queensland, Australia. Lots of sunshine and warm weather. Currawongs are plentiful, as are many other birds. It's a lovely place and we hope to show you more in our games!

entry.title: Gubbi Gubbi
entry.summary: Gubbi Gubbi — the Traditional Custodians of the land where Currawong Games Interactive is based.
entry.content: Gubbi Gubbi

entry.title: Battleboats by Currawong Games Interactive
entry.summary: Battleboats is a friendly competition on the high seas, using realtime controls two players navigate a small map and attempt to outlast each other.
entry.content:
B2! You sunk my battleship!

Battleboats is a top-down, real-time, tactically-chaotic artillery game. You are given command of a battleship with a singular mission: sink all ships in the enemy's fleet. Pick from different classes of ships, go from the meagre battleship to destroyers and aircraft carriers. Deck out the deck with a variety of turrets.

Meet the Boats!
BattleshipDestroyerDreadnoughtAircraft CarrierRowboat
Nimble and fast, the battleship can quickly navigate the battlefield.Balancing speed and firepower, the destroyer is a versatile choice.A large battleship ready to dish out epic damage!Strike from the air and terrorise your opponents!An Old Salt Dog piloting a rowboat with his trusty shotgun. The ultimate challenge for skilled players!

Development

Future plans

  • Larger Battles (10v10)?
  • Different Playstyles (PvP, PvE, Escalation, Customizable!)
  • PvP & PvE
  • Solo Campaign
  • Skins
  • Attach things to the command tower for abilities?
  • Conning Tower
  • False Flag (disguise as enemy ship, or appear damaged?)

Inspired by games we love: Worms, Super Smash Bros, and of course, Battleship.

entry.title: About Currawongs - Currawong Games Interactive
entry.content:
Currawongs

Learn about the Pied Currawong bird species on Wikipedia.

Currawongs are fun birds — you'll often hear them before you see them.

I used to hear Currawongs in Brisbane at dusk, on the walk to the train coming home. Their eerie calls would echo between the buildings. I always wondered what they looked like. It wasn't until they started flying over while going on walks in my neighbourhood that I got to see them.

They look a little like Magpies — also amazing birds, preferably when not swooping — darker, with those bright yellow eyes! And the distinctive calls :)

entry.title: Currawong Games Brand Guidelines
entry.content:
Brand Guidelines

If you're using the Currawong Games brand, please give us a shout at hello@currawong.games.

Logo

Icon

Domain Mark

Colours

Colour Palette (Currawong feathers and eyes)
Colour50100200300400500600700800900950
Currawong
Yellow

Typography

Font: TBD

This website uses system-ui for typography, respecting the visitor's system preferences.

Our "currawong.games" domain mark uses Monaspace Xenon.

entry.title: About Currawong Games Interactive
entry.summary: Currawong Games Interactive is a father-son indie game studio based in Upper Caboolture, Queensland.
entry.content:
About Currawong Games Interactive

We want people to play a Currawong game and immediately want to tell someone about it.

Our Story

Currawong Games Interactive is a father-son indie game studio based in Upper Caboolture, Queensland. We develop story-driven and family-friendly games, interactive narrative experiences, and creator tools — with an Australian flavour. We empower creatives through community participation, tools and transparency.

We offer story-rich, fairly priced games designed for players who value narrative depth and experiences worth sharing with the people they love.

  • Casual players looking for relaxed or story-driven games.
  • Parents and families sharing games/experiences.
  • Fans looking for rich franchises to become engrossed in.
  • Aspiring game developers, artists, creators with similar passions.

Our Goals

To build a sustainable creative practice that supports our family, advances the craft of interactive storytelling, and gives back to the broader creative community — putting Caboolture on the map along the way.

entry.content:
Currawong Games

Something is coming. Stay tuned.

entry.summary: Currawong Games Interactive is a father-son indie game studio based in Upper Caboolture, Queensland.
entry.content:
About Currawong Games Interactive

We want people to play a Currawong game and immediately want to tell someone about it.

Our Story

Currawong Games Interactive is a father-son indie game studio based in Upper Caboolture, Queensland. We develop story-driven and family-friendly games, interactive narrative experiences, and creator tools — with an Australian flavour. We empower creatives through community participation, tools and transparency.

We offer story-rich, fairly priced games designed for players who value narrative depth and experiences worth sharing with the people they love.

  • Casual players looking for relaxed or story-driven games.
  • Parents and families sharing games/experiences.
  • Fans looking for rich franchises to become engrossed in.
  • Aspiring game developers, artists, creators with similar passions.

Our Goals

To build a sustainable creative practice that supports our family, advances the craft of interactive storytelling, and gives back to the broader creative community — putting Caboolture on the map along the way.
